Margaret E. Donnelly
Department of Sociology, Whitfield University
482 Calloway Hall, Providence, RI 02912
m.donnelly@whitfield.edu | (401) 555-0173 | margaretdonnelly.org
Curriculum Vitae — June 2026

Appointments
Whitfield University, Providence, RI
Professor of Sociology, 2019–present
Associate Professor of Sociology (with tenure), 2013–2019
University of Wisconsin–Madison
Assistant Professor of Sociology, 2007–2013

Education
Ph.D., Sociology, Princeton University, 2007
Thesis: Exchange in Intimate Settings: Blood, Organs, and the Moral Economy of Donation
Committee: Paul DiMaggio (chair), Viviana Zelizer, Robert Wuthnow
M.A., Sociology, Princeton University, 2003
B.A. (Mod.), Sociology and Political Science, Trinity College Dublin, 2001

Publications

Books
1. Donnelly, Margaret E. 2021. The Ordinal Society: Rankings and the Quantified Self. Cambridge, MA: Harvard University Press.
2. Donnelly, Margaret E. 2014. Visualizing Social Data: A Practical Introduction. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. Translated into Korean and Spanish.

Journal Articles
3. Donnelly, Margaret E., and Soren Aldridge. 2024. "Seeing Like a Market: Classification Situations and the Moral Economy of Credit Scores." Socio-Economic Review 22(1): 41–68. doi:10.1093/ser/mwad071.
4. Donnelly, Margaret E. 2022. "The Performativity of Networks Reconsidered." European Journal of Sociology 63(2): 175–207. doi:10.1017/S0003975622000133.

Margaret E. Donnelly page 2 of 3

5. Aldridge, Soren, and Margaret E. Donnelly. 2019. "Repugnant Markets and Altruistic Exchange: Organ Donation Regimes in 17 OECD Countries." American Sociological Review 84(3): 512–541. doi:10.1177/0003122419838567.
6. Donnelly, Margaret E. 2016. "Public Sociology in the Age of Social Media." Perspectives on Politics 14(3): 771–780. doi:10.1017/S1537592716001134.

Conference Proceedings
7. Donnelly, Margaret E. 2018. "Counting the Gift: Administrative Data and Donor Registries." Pp. 211–229 in Proceedings of the Annual Meeting of the Eastern Sociological Society, Philadelphia, PA.

Awards and Honors
Clifford Geertz Prize for Best Article, Sociology of Culture Section, ASA, 2023
Lewis A. Coser Award for Theoretical Agenda-Setting, 2017
National Science Foundation CAREER Grant SES-1059089 ($412,000), 2011–2016

Grants and Fellowships
Russell Sage Foundation Visiting Scholar, New York, 2015–2016
Whitfield Humanities Center Faculty Fellowship, 2021

Margaret E. Donnelly page 3 of 3

Service
Editorial Board, American Journal of Sociology, 2018–present
Council Member, ASA Economic Sociology Section, 2020–2023
Reviewer for ASR, AJS, Socio-Economic Review, and Theory and Society
